Andritz Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William J. Rusteen of BULLIVANT HOUSER BAILEY (415-352-2700), appearing for defendant / moving party Andritze Inc.
Amee A. Mikacich of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / joinder Tosco Corporation
Kimberly Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Richard Hartung

RULING:
DEFENDANT ANDRITZ INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS
DEFENDANT TOSCO CORPORATION'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT ANDRITZ INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS

Hearing held. The Court made the following rulings: Defendant Andritz Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that either Oregon or Washington is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431). Defendant's alternate motion to stay the action is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. Defendant Tosco Corporation's joinder in defendant Andritz Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay on grounds of forum non conveniens is moot based on the Court's ruling on the underlying motion. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
